notes: Opening opportunity work notes: inspected current record and list_evidence; no prior party submissions. Read all 11 case-packet items using read_evidence_range. Decisive elements identified: Samantha's representation on completion timing, materiality, falsity/misleading nature, Peter's actual and reasonable reliance, and downstream losses. Strongest proof: deadline-message-thread shows Peter expressly conditioned keeping rush print slot on final package by next evening; Samantha said she had read Stephenson and could deliver in time, then reaffirmed next morning before press approval; Peter confirmed he kept print/distribution plan based on that timing. Print-approval-note contemporaneously records Peter approving rush print because of Samantha's morning confirmation and warns missed window wastes rush setup and distribution. Session-summary is derivative but aligns with chronology. Confession says Samantha had not read the full essay before first draft, implied that she had, had not downloaded complete text when presenting early conclusions, and calls that deceptive/unacceptable; this supports false foundation for assurance, not standing alone the exact missed deadline. Local signature check performed using packet public key and detached signature in /tmp/aar-ex01 with openssl dgst -sha256 -verify samantha_public.pem -signature confession.sig confession.txt; output: Verified OK. Invoices/work order/damages log support reliance/damages context but opening should avoid overclaiming payment/avoidability. Adverse/limits: record still needs tighter proof that final package actually missed the Mar. 11 evening handoff and that Samantha knew the completion assurance was false when made; confession is more direct on misrepresented reading/preparation than on future completion. No external search because packet appears internal/fictional/self-contained; technical authenticity check was material and completed. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Opening defendant notes: inspected get_case/list_evidence; no new submitted evidence beyond 11 case-packet files and plaintiff opening. Read all packet files. Decisive elements: material misrepresentation about completion timing, reasonable reliance, causation/damages. Evidence proves: deadline thread has Samantha saying she can get finished essay/briefing package in time for 2026-03-11 press window and reaffirming completion by tonight; Peter says he will keep rush print/distribution plan in place. Print approval note records Peter's reliance before approving invoice. Invoice/work order show $85k print and $15k distribution commitments. Instructions show duty to read Stephenson before conclusions/drafts. Confession proves Samantha had not read full essay before first draft, implied she had, and had not downloaded complete text when presenting early conclusions. Technical check: reconstructed confession/public key/signature from AAR bytes and ran openssl dgst -sha256 -verify; output Verified OK; confession and public key hashes matched AAR. Defense theory: concede authenticated confession as to preparation dishonesty, but contest proposition's specific leap. The confession does not itself say Samantha lied about when the package would be complete, knew completion was impossible, or missed the evening handoff. The thread is excerpted and lacks full context, delivery outcome, cancellation terms, and proof of actual avoidability/waste. Reasonable reliance can be challenged because Peter approved a commercial print run without final copy in hand and based on his own business risk calculus. Unresolved later targets: full thread, delivery timestamps, printer/distributor cancellation terms, payment/mitigation, final package status, direct state-of-mind evidence.

Merits opportunity work notes: waited through defendant opening, then received arguments:plaintiff. Fresh record scan via get_case/list_evidence: no new evidence; defendant opening added; evidence list unchanged, no submitted evidence or technical reports. Stat checked key evidence: deadline thread, print approval note, confession, Samantha public key. Re-read key evidence in this opportunity: deadline-message-thread, print-approval-note, confession, instructions, printing invoice, distribution work order. Existing local signature verification from opening remains relevant: reconstructed packet confession, signature, and public key in /tmp/aar-ex01; openssl dgst -sha256 -verify samantha_public.pem -signature confession.sig confession.txt returned Verified OK. No outside evidence submitted or relied on because packet evidence is sufficient and appears fictional/internal; no public source target likely to improve proof. Defendant opening themes: deadline assurance exists but argues no proof of falsity when made; confession is preparation-only; reliance unreasonable because Peter approved rush print without final copy. Plaintiff answer: Samantha's completion assurance was tied to the known false factual predicate that she had read Stephenson and was drafting/revising, in response to Peter's explicit conditional go/no-go question; preponderance can find material misrepresentation from the coupled assurance and false preparation status. Reasonable reliance supported by Peter asking first before keeping slot and again before approving invoice, plus Samantha's retained role and the assignment requirement that reading Stephenson was foundational. Evidence plan for filing: offer PX-1 deadline thread, PX-2 print approval note, PX-3 instructions, PX-4 confession, PX-5 signature/public key pair, PX-6 printing invoice, PX-7 distribution work order. Add technical report summarizing signature verification using visible packet evidence. Limitations to acknowledge: no full thread, no delivery timestamp/cancellation terms; use invoices/work order as materiality/reliance context, not overclaim actual recoverable damages.

Argument defendant notes: Fresh get_case/list_evidence scan found same 11 case-packet evidence items and no submitted_evidence; plaintiff added PX-1 through PX-7 from packet plus technical report verifying confession signature. Re-read core evidence this opportunity: deadline thread, confession, print approval note, printing invoice, distribution work order, session summary. Source map: decisive missing targets are full message thread, delivery timestamp/final package status, cancellation/refund terms for print and distribution, actual payment/waste, and direct evidence of Samantha's knowledge when promising completion. External search checked exact identifiers/phrases: "GLP-2026-0311-044", "MDS-2026-0312-118", "I have read Stephenson" "Keep the slot", and "Claws" "essay and client briefing package" "Peter" "Samantha". Results were unrelated GLP-1/MDS/Stephenson/general pages, no source material for transaction; no external evidence submitted or relied on. Analysis: plaintiff's best record proof is timing assurance plus authenticated false preparation predicate. Defense should concede authenticity/preparation dishonesty but argue proposition requires misrepresentation about completion timing. Record lacks proof final package was late, that deadline statement was false when made, full context, cancellation terms, actual waste, and independent reasonableness of Peter's reliance. Planned filing: use only visible packet evidence/offered exhibits; no new evidence; include concise technical report/search ledger.

Rebuttal opportunity work notes: received rebuttals:plaintiff after defendant argument. Fresh record scan via get_case/list_evidence: defendant argument and defendant source-search ledger added; no new evidence beyond same packet items; no submitted external evidence. Read session-summary, deadline thread, and confession in this opportunity. Defendant's strongest points: no delivery log/final timestamp, confession only preparation, Peter's reliance not reasonable because no final package in hand, public search found no missing sources. Planned rebuttal: keep narrow. Argue proposition is material misrepresentation about completion timing, and Samantha's timing assurance was coupled with present factual predicates ('I have read Stephenson' and 'already in drafting and revision') that made the timing credible; confession proves the predicate was false, so the timing assurance was materially misleading when relied on. Delivery timestamp is not required to show the representation was misleading at the moment of reliance. Reasonable reliance supported by Peter asking before keeping slot and asking again before approving invoice; requiring distrust despite direct confirmation would invert reliance law/facts. Defendant source-search ledger only confirms no outside contradiction; it does not displace packet evidence. No new evidence or technical report needed; rely on existing exhibits and technical report.

Surrebuttal defendant notes: Fresh get_case/list_evidence scan shows no newly submitted evidence during rebuttal, same 11 packet items. Re-read deadline thread, confession, and session summary. Plaintiff rebuttal's strongest point is that Samantha's completion assurance had a false present basis because she coupled it with 'I have read Stephenson' and work-status language; plaintiff argues delivery log is unnecessary. Defense response: the false present basis remains false only as to having read Stephenson before early drafts, not direct proof that completion timing was false when made. The phrase 'already in drafting and revision' is not contradicted by the confession; session summary says Samantha produced an early draft and the parties spent more than an hour revising and preparing the package. That supports at least some present work basis. Rebuttal introduces no new source material, only a reframing of same packet, so no new external search or evidence submission is necessary. Planned surrebuttal: concede preparation deception, reject leap to timing falsity, emphasize preponderance and missing direct timing/reliance facts.

Closing opportunity work notes: received closings:plaintiff. Fresh record scan via get_case/list_evidence; defendant surrebuttal is now in record, no new evidence or submitted evidence. Re-read deadline thread, print approval note, confession, and instructions. Defendant surrebuttal's strongest point: session summary shows Samantha had produced an early draft and worked with Peter for over an hour, so plaintiff cannot say deadline assurance had no work basis at all; defense also renews missing delivery log and reasonable reliance arguments. Closing plan: concede record does not prove zero work or include final delivery log; focus on actual proposition and preponderance. Decisive chain: Peter made explicit go/no-go condition; Samantha gave timing assurance coupled with present factual predicates ('read Stephenson' and drafting/revision); Peter relied immediately and again before invoice approval; assignment made reading Stephenson foundational; authenticated confession proves false predicate; approval note and invoices show materiality. Answer surrebuttal by distinguishing some draft work from truthful basis for final completion assurance. Reasonable reliance: Peter's direct confirmations were reasonable because Samantha had superior knowledge of her own source review and drafting status. Closing is record-only; no new evidence or report allowed/needed.

Closing defendant notes: Fresh get_case/list_evidence scan shows same packet and no newly submitted evidence after plaintiff closing. Re-read deadline thread, confession, and session summary. Plaintiff closing's strongest point is central sequence plus false present reading basis; it also concedes record does not show Samantha had done no work. Defense closing should concede authenticated preparation deception but argue proposition remains unproven: record proves timing assurance and reliance narrative, plus false reading statement, but not that promised completion timing was false or materially misleading when made, not that package missed handoff, and not that Peter's rush print reliance was reasonable in context. Key missing proof remains final delivery/late status, what remained to be done at assurance time, knowledge/impossibility, full thread, independent commercial reasonableness, cancellation/avoidability. No new evidence/reports allowed in closing; file from existing record only.
